questions.explanation: because he believed / secondhand smoke / Nelson quit smoking / his children’s health / could harm 解答: Nelson quit smoking because he believed secondhand smoke could harm his children’s health. 評閱指標 級分 說明 所有字詞組合正確,句子通順、完整,句法結 4 構及連貫性甚佳,拼寫及標點完全正確。 多數字詞組合正確,句子通順、完整,句法結 3 構及連貫性佳,但有拼寫或標點錯誤。 僅有部分字詞組合正確,句法結構不良,且缺 2 乏連貫性,或有拼寫、標點錯誤。 多數字詞組合及語法結構錯誤甚多,且缺乏連 1 貫性,或有拼寫、標點錯誤。 (1) 空白 ( 未書寫文字 ) 。 (2) 文字瑣碎、形同未答。 0 (3) 只抄題目或其他試題。 (4) 只抄引導文字。 (5) 內容完全離題或錯誤。 III. 中譯英

questions.explanation: 我深深地相信保護環境是每個人的責任。 解答: I am deeply convinced that / I deeply believe that / I do believe that protecting the environment/environmental protection is everyone’s duty / responsibility. 或 I am deeply convinced that / I deeply believe that / I do believe that it is everyone’s duty / responsibility to protect the environment. 註: everyone / everybody 皆給分 評閱指標 級分 說明 能充分表達原文題意,對題材有絕佳的掌握, 4 句子結構及連貫性甚佳。文法及標點正確,用 字遣詞沒有不當之處。 能適切表達原文題意,對題材有良好的掌握, 3 句子結構及連貫性大致得當。文法及標點、用 字遣詞有少數不當之處,但不妨礙題意之表達。 僅能部分表達原文題意,句子結構不良或有 2 誤,且大多難以理解並缺乏連貫性。文法及標 點、用字遣詞有失當之處。 無法表達原文題意,句子沒有結構概念及連貫 1 性,無法理解。文法及標點、用字遣詞之錯誤 甚多。 (1) 空白 ( 未書寫文字 ) 。 (2) 文字瑣碎、形同未答。 0 (3) 只抄題目或其他試題。 (4) 內容完全離題或錯誤。

questions.q: Jessie: Mike, we need to talk about what to get Mom for Mother’s Day. Mike: Well, a card obviously. And I was thinking probably we could get her a bunch of flowers, like carnations. Jessie: Yeah, but we get her that every year. Mike: I don’t mind getting her something else, but I think we should still get carnations because, you know, it’s a tradition.
options: How about getting her a different gift this year?

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 107-2-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Generally, a planet’s distance from the sun affects the planet’s surface temperatures. As the distance increases, the temperatures decrease. According to this chart, which planet will most likely have the highest temperatures?
options: Mercury ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 34-37 題。 Swimsuit competition has been the focus of the Miss America pageant since 1921 when it was held on the Atlantic City boardwalk. However, what the candidates wore at that time was not bikinis, but one-piece bathing suits, which were conservative by today’s standards. But gradually it became women in bikinis and high heels walking around in the swimsuit competition for attracting a television audience. Over the decades, the Miss America Organization struggled to strike a balance between its mission “empowering women” and its “tradition,” requiring candidates to wear bikinis. In fact, it was hard for audience and people to believe that wearing bikinis has something to do with “empowering women.” Obviously, this assertion was not acceptable for most people. Some former candidates had spoken out against the swimsuit competition, saying it led to serious physical and mental problems. Gretchen Carlson, the new chair of the Miss America Organization, mentioned that she once had talked to tons of young ladies and they told her, “I’d love to be a part of that program, but I don’t want to parade and walk around in a swimsuit.” And Carlson responded on June 5, 2018 on ABC’s Good Morning America, “We are not going to judge you on your outward appearance. We are moving it forward and evolving it in this cultural revolution.” The new competition would definitely focus more on the candidate’s talents, intelligence, and ideas, rather than how she would look in a swimsuit. “So guess what? You don’t have to do that anymore. You’re welcome. Please come join us!” Carlson said. 註: Miss America pageant 美國小姐選美比賽
questions.explanation: 一般而言,行星與太陽的距離影響著行星表面溫度。 隨著距離的增加,溫度就越低。根據這張圖表,哪個 個足球場大。來自世界各地的太空人生活工作在一起。 星球的溫度有可能最高? 這裡的培訓計劃涵蓋了許多學科,從天文學、地質學到 攝影。美國太空培訓官員塔瑪拉約克說道: 「太空人也會 (A) 地球 (B) 木星 (C) 火星 (D) 水星 Question 34-37 學習他們在太空中所該進行的所有科學實驗」 。 自 1921 年第一次在大西洋城海濱大道上舉行比賽以 太空人面臨最困難的任務之一就是到太空站外進行 來,泳裝比賽一直是美國小姐選美大賽的焦點。然而, 維修。練習如何在太空中走路是培訓最重要的部分。為 當初候選人所穿的並不是比基尼,而是連身泳衣,按照 此,太空人需花時間在休士頓的「中立浮力實驗室」學 今天的標準看來相當保守。不過,漸漸的,泳裝比賽轉 習。這實驗室可稱是世界上最大的室內游泳池,條件與 為女性穿著比基尼和高跟鞋在比賽時走台步,吸引電視 太空中相似。太空人潛水時,會穿著他們在太空漫步時 觀眾。 穿的太空服。 幾十年來,美國小姐協會一直想要在它「賦予女性 「緊急事情都會發生,而太空人需要做好準備」,約 權力」的使命和它要求參賽者穿著比基尼的「傳統」間 克說道。各種緊急情況在太空中都可能發生,這就是為 取得平衡。事實上,觀眾和人們很難相信穿著比基尼與 什麼培訓都聚焦在如何處理可能出現在太空中的問題。 「賦予女性權力」有什麼關係。顯然地,這種的說法對 例如,太空人必須學會如果發生火災該怎麼辦。阿諾德 大多數人來說是不可接受的。一些前任參賽者已經發表 甚至在醫院急診室完成了一回合訓練,因為「如果你需 聲明,反對泳裝比賽,認為這導致了嚴重的身心問題。 要縫合傷口,那麼將是你或你的夥伴為你做這件事」,阿 美國小姐協會的新主席格雷琴卡爾森提到,她曾經 諾德說道。 和很多年輕女士暢談過,她們告訴她, 「我很想參加,但

options: She was the new leader of Miss America Organization.
She openly invited those who were interested in Miss America but didn’t want to wear a swimsuit to join the contest.
For her, the contestants’ talents and intelligence are less important than their outward appearance.
She was ready to create a different, innovative Miss America.

questions.q: What was people’s attitude toward the mission of the Miss America Organization, “empowering women,” when the swimsuit competition was still the focus?
questions.explanation: 當泳裝仍為比賽焦點時,人們對美國小姐協會的使命 訓練。 「賦予女性權力」的態度是什麼? (D) 太空人在國際空間站外接受太空漫步訓練。 (A) 懷疑的。 (B) 有希望的。 ( 根據第三段,太空行走訓練是在「中立浮力實驗室」 (C) 可接受的。 (D) 樂觀的。 學習 )

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 107-2-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Which of the following is true of Miss America’s swimsuit competition?
options: In the competition of 2018, the candidates would wear one-piece bathing suits.
What the candidates wore in the very beginning of Miss America pageant was fashionable by today’s standards.
From 1921, high heels with bikinis had become the feature of Miss America pageant.

Miss America pageant in 2018 would reconsider the necessity of the swimsuit competition. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-41 題。 The life of astronauts in the space station is never easy. In fact, it can be very unpredictable. There is no gravity. To get ready for all the possibilities in space, astronauts should go through a tough training program. As NASA astronaut Ricky Arnold mentions, “We train every day. And things can change quickly up there. We need to be prepared.” The ISS (The International Space Station) is a giant flying space laboratory operated by the United States, Russia, Japan, Europe, and Canada. It is the size of a football field. Astronauts from around the world can live and work together. The training program covers many subjects, from astronomy, geology to photography. “Astronauts also learn about all the science experiments they will be doing in space,” says Tamara York, a training officer at NASA. One of the most difficult tasks for astronauts is going outside the station to do repairs. Practicing for how to walk in space is a key part of training. Therefore, astronauts spend time in Neutral Buoyancy Lab in Houston. It is the world’s largest indoor pool. The conditions there are close to those in space. While diving, astronauts wear the space suit they would wear on a spacewalk. “Very critical things can occur, and astronauts need to be ready,” says York. All kinds of emergencies can happen in space and that is the reason why the training focuses on preparing for problems that might come up in space. For instance, an astronaut must learn what to do if there is a fire. Arnold has even completed the training at a hospital emergency room because “If you need stitches, it is going to be you or your buddies doing it for you,” Arnold says. 註 (1): The International Space Station:國際太空站,縮寫為 ISS 註 (2): Neutral Buoyancy Lab:中立浮力實驗室
questions.explanation: 下列對於美國小姐的泳裝比賽的敘述,何者正確? 第二部分:非選擇題 (A) 在 2018 年的比賽中,候選人將穿著連身泳衣。 (B) 最初美國小姐候選人的穿著,以現今的標準來 I. 填充 看,是相當時尚的。
